key_insight: A graph-based spatio-temporal model learns breast MRI treatment trajectories to predict pathological complete response after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
clinical_relevance: This could support earlier, individualized treatment-response stratification in breast cancer, helping identify patients more likely to benefit from standard neoadjuvant therapy.
method: Built a 3D spatio-temporal framework using a graph neural network to model relationships across MRI timepoints.
Added three self-supervised representation learning objectives to learn richer treatment-trajectory features.
Evaluated the effect of scan count per patient and inter-scan timing on prediction performance.
evidence_signal: On the public ISPY-2 cohort of 585 patients, the method reportedly outperformed vision and self-supervised baselines across several classification metrics.
watch_item: Performance depends on longitudinal DCE-MRI availability, so missing or unevenly spaced timepoints may limit real-world deployment and comparability across sites.
paper_title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction
